title: Student Disability Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Student Disability Services 23 Matching Service Providers MALHEUR EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION SERVICES AND PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Student Services/Special Education Department at Malheur Education Service District Region 14 helps school districts support students with unique learning needs. Referral, diagnosis, consultation, and direct treatment therapy services are provided. 363 A Street W Vale, OR 97918 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 473-4821 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK BEND OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 2525 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 7 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK KLAMATH FALLS OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 2701 Foothills Boulevard Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK WARM SPRINGS OFFICE DISABILITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational parenting support, family support group sessions, education and mentoring support, and referral to other resources as needed. This empowers individuals with disabilities, and their families, to take charge of their life's direction. Also assists in determining supports, goals, and community inclusion, resulting in increased independence. 1257 Kot-Num Road Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of services for children with special needs and the school districts serving them, including IEP support and transition services. 13455 SE 97th Avenue Clackamas, OR 97015 Eligibility: Children with special needs Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 675-4260 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DEPARTMENT OF EARLY LEARNING AND CARE DEPARTMENT OF EARLY LEARNING AND CARE CHILD CARE INCLUSIVE PARTNERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Inclusive Partners is a statewide program that supports inclusive practices in child care settings. Works with child care providers serving children who need additional accommodations. Offers technical assistance, consultation, financial supplements, and professional development to promote and sustain inclusive care. Inclusive Partners works with ODHS Self-Sufficiency to coordinate the High Needs program for families with children who have higher needs and are receiving child care subsidy through ERDC or JOBS/TANF. Local ODHS workers, not Inclusive Partners staff, determine eligibility for child care assistance through ERDC or TANF/JOBS. Inclusive Partners staff complete the High Needs Rate Determination Assessment in the child care setting to determine the child’s individual rate and the provider’s consultation and technical assistance needs. Website Eligibility: Providers: Unrestricted Children needing care: Ages 0-17 with physical, developmental, mental, emotional, behavioral, or medical disabilities who require extra support or accommodations to be in child care and who are receiving child care subsidy through Department of Early Learning and Care (ERDC or JOBS/TANF) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 673-2286 Visit Website More Details DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of services for children with special needs and the school districts serving them, including Individualized Education Program (IEP) support and transition services. 1409 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Suite 110 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 440-4763 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIND CHILDCARE OREGON FIND CHILD CARE OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps families locate child care through referrals and provides resources to help make informed decisions about child care needs. 211info is available to provide families referrals to support their child care search, or families can conduct their own search on the website. & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for children with vision impairment, hearing impairment, severe orthopedic impairment, and autism spectrum disorder. Collaborates with component school districts and programs to provide specialized support. Services includes consultation, assessment, instruction, and adaptive or specialized materials and equipment, as well as assistance with IEP development and implementation. 905 4th Avenue SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Must contact school's special education director to start the referral process and determine eligibility. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 812-2770 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of services for children with special needs and the school districts serving them, including IEP support and transition services. 11611 NE Ainsworth Circle Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Children with special needs Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 257-1679 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT AUTISM SPECTRUM DISORDER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides licensed special education teachers with specific teaching experience, training, and knowledge in the area of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). These services support districts as they work to build the capacity of their special education teams. Examples of this work include developing staff knowledge of the characteristics of ASD, classroom-based strategies, evidence-based practices (EBPs), and assessment requirements and procedures. 5825 NE Ray Circle Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Ages 5-21, medical or health assessment is required as part of the educational eligibility process Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 614-1310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT NORTHWEST REGIONAL EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT CLATSOP SERVICE CENTER AUTISM SPECTRUM DISORDER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides licensed special education teachers with specific teaching experience, training, and knowledge in the area of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). These services support districts as they work to build the capacity of their special education teams. Examples of this work include developing staff knowledge of the characteristics of ASD, classroom-based strategies, evidence-based practices (EBPs), and assessment requirements and procedures. 785 Alameda Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Ages 5-21, medical or health assessment is required as part of the educational eligibility process Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 338-3358 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of services for children with special needs, and the school districts serving them, including IEP support and transition services. Also offers Augmentative and Alternative Communication, a Feeding Team, a Crisis Prevention Institute, and a Special Education Director. 1350 Teakwood Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 269-1611 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides evaluative, consultative, and direct special education services to students with disabilities, including assistive technology, audiological, autism, deaf and hard of hearing, long-term care and treatment, visual, psychological, orthopedic, speech and language, and STEPS (students with severe and/or multiple disabilities). Also provides evaluation and special education services to children from birth to years. 101 N Grape Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 776-8590 3110 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WALLOWA EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT REGION 18 SPECIAL EDUCATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supervision and coordination for staffing the resource rooms in school districts. Offers Individualized Education Program (IEP) review, vision and hearing screening, developmental assessment, and supports for students with disabilities. 107 SW First Street Suite 105 Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 426-7600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILLAMETTE EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT WILLAMETTE ESD MARION CENTER SPECIAL EDUCATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers direct instruction and related services to children and students with special needs, their families and school districts. 2611 Pringle Road SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Special education programs provide instruction to students in grades K-12, ages 5-21. Referral and placement decisions are based on recommendations by the IEP team, which includes the child's family.
